answer:
Based on the context, Diane will know the hit man has killed Camilla when she finds **the blue key**—the same one linked to Rita’s purse and the mysterious box—left behind as proof of the job.

The hit man explicitly tells her, *"When the job is done, you will find a blue key,"* implying it serves as confirmation of Camilla’s death.

The key’s eerie significance (tying to the blue box and Club Silencio’s illusions) suggests it’s the only tangible clue in this fractured, dreamlike narrative.
